_meta.note: CURATED SEED SET of real, currently-contested projects for demonstration. Coordinates are approximate (county/site centroids). Impact ratings are transparent editorial assessments (1-5), not authoritative. In production this file is overwritten by harvest_projects.py running on GitHub Actions, which pulls from open sources (EPA EIS, FERC, Global Energy Monitor, EJAtlas, Land Matrix, municipal open-data permit portals).
_meta.rating_scale: 1 minor · 2 local · 3 regional · 4 major · 5 landscape/nationally significant

projects.name: Mountain Valley Pipeline
projects.desc: A 303-mile fracked-gas pipeline crossing hundreds of Appalachian streams and steep karst terrain. Southgate extension into NC still contested. WHY RUN TO IT: water crossings, erosion violations and the Southgate permit are live pressure points.

projects.name: Thacker Pass Lithium Mine
projects.desc: The largest lithium mine in the US, on land sacred to Paiute-Shoshone people and sage-grouse habitat. WHY RUN TO IT: ongoing ESA/NHPA litigation and tribal-consultation fights.

projects.name: Enbridge Line 5 (Straits of Mackinac)
projects.desc: A 70-year-old oil pipeline on the bottom of the Great Lakes, plus a proposed tunnel. WHY RUN TO IT: state permits, tribal treaty rights and the Army Corps review are all open fronts.

projects.name: Calcasieu Pass 2 (CP2 LNG)
projects.desc: A massive LNG export terminal in Cameron Parish wetlands — a flashpoint of the Gulf LNG buildout. WHY RUN TO IT: FERC and DOE export authorizations are contested; comment windows open.

projects.name: Formosa Plastics ‘Sunshine Project’
projects.desc: A giant petrochemical complex in St. James Parish (‘Cancer Alley’), on land including likely enslaved-people burial sites. WHY RUN TO IT: air-permit challenges and environmental-justice review are active.

projects.name: Willow Project
projects.desc: A major oil development in the National Petroleum Reserve-Alaska on the Arctic Slope. WHY RUN TO IT: ongoing litigation over the BLM approval and caribou/subsistence impacts.

projects.name: Atlanta Public Safety Training Center (‘Cop City’)
projects.desc: A police training campus cleared from the Weelaunee/South River Forest. WHY RUN TO IT: land-use permits, a referendum fight and forest-defense organizing are ongoing.

projects.name: Resolution Copper Mine (Oak Flat)
projects.desc: A block-cave copper mine that would swallow Oak Flat, sacred to the San Carlos Apache. WHY RUN TO IT: the federal land exchange and its EIS are in active litigation.

projects.name: Pebble Mine
projects.desc: A proposed mine in the headwaters of Bristol Bay, the world’s largest sockeye salmon fishery. WHY RUN TO IT: developers keep seeking to overturn the EPA Clean Water Act veto.
